preamble: The shift from the diphthong [ai] to a nasalized vowel [ã] involves both smoothing of the diphthong and nasalization of the resulting vowel, typically in environments where a nasal consonant was present or as a dialectal simplification.

examples.note: Stages of nasal diphthong development and simplification in Portuguese historical phonology.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
